data.title: Buckle sets $2.50 special cash dividend for January
data.description:
The Buckle (NYSE:BKE) announced on Tuesday that its board of directors authorized a $2.50 per share special cash dividend to be paid to shareholders of record at the close of business on January 15. The board also authorized a $0.35 per share quarterly dividend to be paid to shareholders of record at the close of business on January 15.

Both the $2.50 per share special cash dividend and the $0.35 per share quarterly dividend ($2.85 per share in total) are payable on January 29, and will be paid together.

Of note, The retailer matched the $2.50 special dividend paid out on Janutry 12, 2024, and did not change its dividend payout rate.

Shares of Buckle (BKE) are up 15% over the last six weeks and traded very close to the 52-week share price high.

data.title: AI/ML Innovations to Acquire Quantum Sciences for $690K
data.description:
• AI/ML Innovations (OTCQB:AIMLF) has announced a share purchase agreement to acquire Quantum Sciences for $690K.

  • Quantum designs digital health care solutions using artificial intelligence, machine learning, and quantum-inspired methodologies. They aim to improve patient and care provider participation, enhancing workflow efficiency and enhancing healthcare trajectories.

  • The consideration amount will be paid through the issuance of 10 million common shares at $0.069 per share, reflecting a 25% discount to the 20-day VWAP on the Canadian Securities Exchange.

  • The transaction is subject to CSE approval and customary closing conditions, with no new control persons being created and no significant debt being assumed.

More on AI/ML Innovations:

data.title: West Virginia to give $10M funding for Babcock & Wilcox project
data.description:
• Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises (NYSE:BW) said its unit will get funding of up to $10 million from the West Virginia Department of Economic Development for the development of a BrightLoop hydrogen production and carbon capture facility in Mason County.
  • The memorandum of agreement between the unit and the state has performance-based financial and economic development assistance for the development of a low-carbon-emissions hydrogen production facility.
  • The facility uses B&W’s BrightLoop chemical looping technology and is powered by coal and biomass.
  • The funding will be phased in upon achievement of project milestones.

data.title: Alaska Air Group launches new global gateway in Seattle as integration of Hawaiian Airlines continues
data.description:
Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK) announced that it is marking another step forward in the combination of Alaska Airlines and Hawaiian Airlines by launching a new global gateway in Seattle.

The Seattle gateway will include new nonstop service with Hawaiian Airlines widebody aircraft between Seattle and Tokyo Narita, Japan, and Seoul Incheon, South Korea on Hawaiian's Airbus A330-200 aircraft, beginning next year. An enhanced domestic network for the two airlines will launch this spring.

The company noted that the Seattle hub is the largest on the West Coast of any carrier, serving 104 nonstop destinations across North America, allowing for convenient travel options and connections. Including Tokyo Narita and Seoul Incheon, the company plans to serve at least 12 nonstop global destinations with long-haul widebody aircraft from our Seattle gateway by 2030, with further specifics on where and when to be revealed later.

"Our new widebody service between Seattle and Tokyo Narita enables us to right-size our capacity between Hawaiʻi and Japan, a market that has experienced a weaker leisure travel demand in the wake of the pandemic," noted Alaska Air (ALK) CEO Ben Minicucci. "As we reposition the aircraft on the Honolulu-Tokyo Narita route to Seattle, we will offer more flying options between Honolulu and Tokyo Haneda – one of two international airports in Japan's capital – by increasing Hawaiian's service from 12 to 14 flights a week," he added.

Hawaiian will also continue to offer nonstop service between Honolulu and both Osaka and Fukuoka for a total of 24 weekly roundtrips between Hawaii and Japan.

data.title: RELX division to buy IDVerse to enhance capability
data.description:
• LexisNexis Risk Solutions, part of RELX (NYSE:RELX) to acquire IDVerse, a provider of AI-powered automated document authentication and fraud detection solutions.
  • IDVerse will become part of LexisNexis Risk Solutions Business Services. Founded in Australia and launched commercially in 2018, IDVerse uses AI to combat fraud and deepfakes.
  • The closing of the transaction is expected in early 2025, other details were not disclosed.
  • "AI-powered solutions are necessary to counter the threat of AI-generated fraud attacks, including deepfakes. Integrating IDVerse's advanced and complementary technology will further enhance our ability to provide the risk insights our clients need to defend against bad actors today and into the future – regardless of where our clients are in the world or where they do business." commented Rick Trainor, CEO of Business Services for LexisNexis Risk Solutions.

data.title: ChipMOS revenue dips 2.1% Y/Y in November
data.description:
• ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES (NASDAQ:IMOS) reported a 2.1% Y/Y decline in revenue for the month of Nov. 2024.
  • Revenue amounted to US$56.4M in the month, up 2.8% from Oct., but down 2.1% from Nov. 2023.
  • The company noted growth (sequential) was led by a further stabilization in memory and a continued normalization in customer inventory levels.
